note: seq=74 (PARK CT cleanly -- stop the flap). atlas-ct-multilog keeps failing (exit-code, streak) because the box egress to the Google/Cloudflare CT get-entries endpoints is 403-blocked (no path), and the new watchdog kept reviving it every 15 min = churn. NRD (seq-72, Whoisds free daily) already covers the same-day business-birth need with real rows, so CT is parked cleanly rather than left flapping: stop + disable + MASK both atlas-ct-multilog.timer and .service (masked units cannot be started, so the watchdog skips them), reset-failed, and drop a /var/lib/atlas/ct_parked marker. Reversible (systemctl unmask) if box egress to CT ever opens. NOT a stop/disable of ssh or atlas-autopull (allowlist/guardrail clean).

steps.note: seq=74: park atlas-ct-multilog (mask) -- egress-blocked, NRD covers it; stop the watchdog flap.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import subprocess as s,json,os,time; [s.run(['systemctl',a,u]) for u in ('atlas-ct-multilog.timer','atlas-ct-multilog.service') for a in ('stop','disable','mask')]; s.run(['systemctl','reset-failed','atlas-ct-multilog.service']); os.makedirs('/var/lib/atlas',exist_ok=True); open('/var/lib/atlas/ct_parked','w').write('parked: box egress to CT logs is 403-blocked; NRD covers same-day-birth. '+time.ctime()); print('CT parked (masked); watchdog can no longer revive it')"
